# Performance Checklist: Hybrid Semantic Search

**Purpose**: Requirements-quality validation ("unit tests for English") for the
performance-relevant requirements of SPEC-003 — fusion-compute latency gate,
matrix-cache build/staleness cost, keyword-arm non-regression, embed-budget
bounding, and CI determinism of the p95 gate.
**Created**: 2026-07-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) · [plan.md](../plan.md) · [research.md](../research.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist performance`. Each item tests whether a
performance requirement is present, clear, measurable, consistent, and complete —
NOT whether the implementation meets it.

## p95 Fusion-Compute Gate — Measurement Method

- [ ] CHK001 Is the p95 fusion-compute latency target quantified with a specific threshold and node scale? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008/SC-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Is the measured leg of "fusion compute" enumerated exactly (vector scan + top-k + RRF merge) so the boundary of what is timed is unambiguous? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008/§D5]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the p95 measurement protocol fully specified — iteration count (N=200), warmup discard (10), percentile rule (nearest-rank), assertion count (single), and retry policy (none)? [Measurability, Spec §FR-014c]
- [ ] CHK004 Is the percentile index computation internally consistent and unambiguous (nearest-rank `sorted[Math.ceil(0.95*200)-1]` = `sorted[189]`)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014c]
- [ ] CHK005 Is the latency fixture's generation method specified deterministically (seeded pure-JS PRNG, documented seed, no committed binary, no `Math.random`)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014c/§D11]
- [ ] CHK006 Are the gated vs reported-only dimensions specified consistently (50k×384 gated; 3584 reported not gated)? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions/SC-002]

## p95 Gate — CI-Stability & Determinism (flake-resistance)

- [x] CHK007 Is the expected/observed fusion-compute p95 and the headroom margin below the 150 ms threshold quantified, so a single no-retry assertion cannot flake on a noisy shared CI runner? [Resolved →FR-014d] (order-of-magnitude headroom + re-measure-if-within-2× rule)
- [~] CHK008 Is a reference hardware / CI-runner class (or a normalization strategy) documented for the absolute-millisecond gate, given that raw timings are hardware-dependent? [Resolved-partial →FR-014d; consensus] (GitHub-hosted runner class named + headroom absorbs variance; whether formal normalization is needed → consensus [domain])
- [x] CHK009 Are measurement-isolation requirements defined to insulate the 200-iteration timing from CI noise (GC pauses, CPU steal, concurrent load in the same process)? [Resolved →FR-014d]
- [x] CHK010 Is a flake/failure policy stated for a single noisy breach under the "no retry" rule — how a one-off environmental breach is distinguished from a genuine performance regression? [Resolved →FR-014d] (order-of-magnitude headroom ⇒ a breach signals a real regression; within-2× triggers pre-merge revisit)
- [ ] CHK011 Is the rationale for N=200 iterations and the fixed 10-iteration warmup discard documented as sufficient to stabilize JIT/allocation for the Float32Array cosine loop? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014c]

## Matrix Cache — Build & Staleness Cost

- [x] CHK012 Is the matrix-cache **build cost** on the first semantic query quantified or bounded, and its exclusion from the gated fusion-compute leg stated explicitly? [Resolved →FR-008a]
- [x] CHK013 Is the per-query **staleness-probe cost** quantified — "cheap" is not measurable — and is it stated whether the probe is inside or outside the ≤150 ms fusion-compute gate? [Resolved →FR-008b] (bounded count + scalar reads, outside the gate)
- [x] CHK014 Is the matrix **rebuild** cost after a staleness-probe invalidation mid-session addressed (not only the first cold build), including rebuild-thrash behavior under a rapidly changing index? [Resolved →FR-008a]
- [ ] CHK015 Is resident memory quantified with the `count×dims×4B` formula and worked examples across the real envelope (dogfood ≈64 MB, bundled 50k×384 ≈77 MB, 50k×3584 ≈717 MB corner)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009/§D6]
- [x] CHK016 Is the single-owner memory invariant (one resident matrix per project in the main daemon process, never duplicated per query worker) captured in the spec, not only in research? [Resolved →FR-009a]
- [~] CHK017 Is a memory guard / graceful-degradation requirement defined for when the matrix would exceed available memory at the 717 MB corner (fall back to keyword vs. risk OOM)? [Resolved-partial →FR-009c; consensus] (v1 documents no-guard known-limitation; whether a resident-size guard is needed → consensus [codebase, domain])

## Keyword-Arm Non-Regression

- [x] CHK018 Is a keyword-mode **latency non-regression** requirement stated — no cache build, no embed call, and no added per-query staleness probe on the keyword path — distinct from the byte-identical *output* assertion? [Resolved →FR-003a]
- [ ] CHK019 Are internal callers (explore, prompt hook, context builder) required to incur zero query-embed latency, and is that asserted measurably (zero calls on the provider seam)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003/FR-014]
- [ ] CHK020 Is byte-identical keyword output specified as verifiable (structural deep-equal plus explicit new-field-absence checks for `matchType`/fused score)? [Measurability, Spec §FR-014/SC-004]

## Embed-Leg Budget & Degraded-Path Latency

- [ ] CHK021 Is the per-query embed budget pinned to an exact internal constant rather than "approximately 2 seconds," given the requirement that no query blocks past the budget? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK022 Is the embed-budget cap itself objectively measurable (the timing bound is enforced), not only the keyword-fallback *output* it produces on timeout? [Resolved →FR-006a]
- [x] CHK023 Is user-perceived latency under degraded conditions bounded — e.g., warming/timeout worst case = keyword latency + at most the embed budget — so the degraded path has a stated latency ceiling? [Resolved →FR-006a]
- [ ] CHK024 Is the query-embed leg's reporting defined as non-gating with a specified measurement/render surface (footer + CLI `--json`), and omitted under keyword/degraded modes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]

## Concurrency

- [x] CHK025 Are performance requirements defined for concurrent first-query matrix builds (thundering-herd: is the lazy build serialized behind the single owner, or can parallel queries each trigger a build)? [Resolved →FR-009b]
- [ ] CHK026 Is contention on the single main-process matrix owner addressed for low-QPS semantic search (a ≤150 ms main-loop step blocking other work), and the SharedArrayBuffer scale-up path named as a follow-up? [Assumption, Spec §D7]

## Consistency & Traceability

- [ ] CHK027 Do the p95 method statements in FR-014c, SC-002, and research §D11 agree exactly (N=200, warmup 10, `sorted[189]`, no retry)? [Consistency]
- [ ] CHK028 Does the memory envelope in research §D6 (64/77/717 MB) reconcile with the spec Edge Cases "717 MB" corner without contradiction? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases/§D6]
- [ ] CHK029 Is the assumption that the brute-force scan meets the p95 target at 50k×384 documented with a validating basis rather than asserted? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK030 Are the performance-relevant constants (embed budget, candidate depth `max(5×limit,100)`, RRF `k=60`) each documented as internal constants with exact values and no new config surface? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004/FR-006/FR-007]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- A `Gap` marker (bracketed) flags a missing/insufficient requirement; count via runner helper `count-markers`.
- Items reference spec sections `[Spec §X]`, research decisions `[§Dn]`, or quality markers.
